title: Indian rupee in free fall: What the govt is doing to lure foreign capital and lift currency
summary: India's rupee is under pressure, hitting record lows against the US dollar due to economic uncertainty and high import bills. The government and the RBI have implemented several measures to stabilise the currency and attract foreign inflows.&nbsp;

title: RBI announces redemption price for SGB 2021-22 Series III, investors set for over 220% gains; check details
summary: The RBI has fixed the premature redemption price at ₹15,512 per unit for SGB 2021-22 Series III due on 8 June 2026.

title: Do you have to pay tax on your NRE deposit interest? Key rules NRIs should know
summary: NRE accounts allow NRIs to deposit foreign income in Indian rupees, facilitating free repatriation. Interest on NRE fixed deposits is exempt from Indian income tax, enhancing their appeal.

title: Nifty Midcap150 Momentum 50: This index combines mid-cap potential with momentum factor
summary: Nifty Midcap150 Momentum 50 Index includes 50 mid-cap stocks that have delivered the strongest momentum, thereby combining the growth potential of mid-caps with the momentum factor's ability to identify market leaders.

title: CAGR vs XIRR: Which is better for analysing mutual fund returns?
summary: The Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) is the average annual rate of return of a lump-sum investment over a specific period. The Extended Internal Rate of Return (XIRR) is a return calculation method used for investments with multiple cash flows occurring on different dates.

title: A guide to government securities for retail investors: Meaning, ways to invest, benefits and risks
summary: Government securities (G-Secs) are debt instruments issued by the RBI on behalf of the central and state governments to raise funds from investors. These securities can be purchased in the primary market during government auctions or in the secondary market.&nbsp;

title: HDFC, ICICI Prudential and Nippon are restricting gold ETF inflows. Here's why
summary: Gold ETF inflows are at record highs, but fund houses are becoming more selective about fresh subscriptions. Here's why the rupee, gold imports and bullion supply are driving the decision.

title: ITR filing AY 2026-27: How to claim TDS refund while filing your income tax return
summary: Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) is a way through which tax is collected in advance by deducting a certain amount from specified payments. A TDS refund becomes applicable when the amount of tax deducted from your income during a financial year is more than the tax you are actually required to pay.

title: Mango bonds and tree renting: a side of Alphonso in your portfolio
summary: Consumers are renting mango trees and buying so-called mango bonds to secure premium fruit directly from farms. But are these genuine investments or simply a smarter way to buy mangoes?

title: US stocks: S&P 500, Nasdaq end up as tech, chipmakers rebound
summary: U.S. stocks largely rose Monday, with the Nasdaq and chipmakers leading the gains as investors sought bargains after Friday's selloff. Relief over Iran and Israel halting attacks also boosted sentiment. Despite Apple's AI upgrades, its shares eased, while Intel surged on a reported Google order.

title: Government to sell 3% stake in NLC India through OFS, sets floor price at Rs 303
summary: The government has launched a two-day offer for sale in NLC India, aiming to divest up to 3% stake through a base offer and greenshoe option. The issue, priced at a discount to the market rate, could raise up to Rs 1,263 crore and forms part of the broader disinvestment programme.

title: US stocks today: US market gains as semiconductor stocks rebound, Middle East tensions ease
summary: Major Wall Street indexes saw a strong rebound. This recovery was driven by gains in chip stocks. Signs of de-escalation in Middle East tensions also provided support. The Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all registered gains at the opening bell.

title: Global Markets: UK financial regulator softens proposed change to UK money market fund rules
summary: In May the government said it would ‌impose tougher ⁠rules ⁠on money market funds, which were hit by heavy redemptions during a ​COVID-19-induced "dash for cash" and have been a focus of regulators since.
